Container.Name: claude-code-dock
Container.Overview:
Run Claude Code (@anthropic-ai/claude-code) persistently on a 24/7 server. Boots inside a tmux session so it survives disconnects and restarts, and reconnects via the Console tab (Shell field: claude-console) or SSH. Credentials, workspace, and settings persist in bind-mounted paths across container restarts and updates. Each container is one isolated session — add the container again under a different name to run a second project with its own workspace, REMOTE_SESSION_NAME, and Claude Config path. Want both to share one Claude login instead of logging in twice? Set the same host path in both containers' "Shared Credentials" field below, rather than pointing both at the same Claude Config path directly (that would alias the entire session directory -- history, settings, cache -- between two live containers at once, not just the login).
Requires a one-time interactive login on first start: open the Console (or attach via SSH) and follow Claude Code's own authentication prompt. No credentials are ever handled by this template.

Config.Name: Workspace
Config.Description: Your project files. Prefer cache/SSD over the array for I/O-heavy work.

Config.Name: Claude Config
Config.Description: This session's Claude Code credentials/settings (equivalent to CONFIG_BASE_PATH/REMOTE_SESSION_NAME in the Docker Compose setup). Use a distinct subfolder per container -- never point two containers at the same path.

Config.Name: Global Config (optional)
Config.Description: Optional: a folder with a global CLAUDE.md, commands/, and skills/ applied to every session. Leave the host path empty/unused if you don't need this.

Config.Name: Shared Credentials (optional)
Config.Description: Optional: point two or more containers at this SAME host path to share one Claude Code login instead of each logging in separately. The first container to log in seeds it; the others load from it automatically (a login/refresh in any one reaches the others within a few seconds). Leave empty/unused for a fully isolated session. Doesn't need to exist beforehand.

Config.Name: AUTO_START_MODE
Config.Description: interactive (Claude in a terminal, default), remote (Claude Remote Control as the main process), or shell (debugging).

Config.Name: REMOTE_SESSION_NAME
Config.Description: Required. Unique name for this session -- shown in startup logs and used as the tmux/remote-control session identifier. Two containers must never share this value.

Config.Name: CLAUDE_EXTRA_ARGS
Config.Description: Extra arguments appended to the claude command, e.g. --model sonnet. Quote-aware: a quoted substring with spaces survives as one argument, e.g. --append-system-prompt "be terse".

Config.Name: GitHub Token File (optional)
Config.Description: Path to a plain-text file on the host containing only your GitHub Personal Access Token (scope: repo), for git push/pull authentication. Create the file yourself first via the Unraid terminal, e.g.: echo -n ghp_xxx > /mnt/user/appdata/claude-code-dock/github_token, then chmod 600 on it. Leave this field empty for public-repo read-only or SSH-based workflows -- Claude Code simply won't have git push credentials. The token's value is never stored in this template or in a container env var, only in this file.

Config.Name: GIT_REPO_URL
Config.Description: HTTPS URL of a repo to auto-clone into /workspace on first start, only if the workspace is empty (optional).
